title: Subcommands -- session, auth, mcp, doctor, completion
purpose: The CLI's subcommands provide essential non-conversational operations: session management (list, show, resume, fork, export, remove), authentication (login, logout, status), MCP server mode, system health checks, and shell completion generation. Without these, users cannot manage sessions outside the REPL, authenticate, expose Norn as an MCP server, diagnose setup problems, or generate shell completions. Each subcommand is a thin handler that delegates to libnorn APIs.
task: Replace the stub subcommand handlers from NC-001 with real implementations, organized as a folder module at crates/norn-cli/src/commands/ with session.rs, auth.rs, mcp.rs, doctor.rs, and completion.rs. Session subcommands read and manipulate the JSONL session store (from NC-002). Auth subcommands delegate to norn::provider::auth::login() and logout(), and inspect Norn-owned $NORN_HOME/auth/auth.json (default ~/.norn/auth/auth.json) for status. MCP serve delegates to norn::integration::mcp_server::McpServer::serve_stdio() with the full tool registry. MCP connect performs an initialize handshake and tools/list against an external server. Doctor checks auth status, provider connectivity, and working directory permissions. Completion uses clap_complete to generate scripts for bash, zsh, and fish.

requirements.title: Commands folder module structure
requirements.spec: The system SHALL organize subcommand handlers as a folder module at crates/norn-cli/src/commands/ with mod.rs containing only pub mod declarations and re-exports. Each subcommand group SHALL have its own file: session.rs, auth.rs, mcp.rs, doctor.rs, completion.rs.
acceptance: crates/norn-cli/src/commands/mod.rs contains only pub mod and pub use statements
Each handler file exists and compiles
No handler file exceeds 500 lines of code

requirements.title: Session list subcommand
requirements.spec: WHEN the user runs 'norn session list', the system SHALL read the session index and display sessions from the current working directory, sorted newest-first. IF --all is set, the system SHALL show sessions from all directories. IF --limit is set, the system SHALL display at most that many sessions. IF --format is 'json', the system SHALL output a JSON array to stdout. IF --format is 'table' (default), the system SHALL output a formatted table with columns: ID (first 8 characters), name, model, turns, updated, working directory.
acceptance: 'norn session list' shows sessions filtered to the current directory
'norn session list --all' shows all sessions
'norn session list --limit 5' shows at most 5 sessions
'norn session list --format json' outputs valid JSON array to stdout
Sessions are sorted newest-first by updated timestamp
When no sessions exist, the table output shows a 'No sessions found.' message

requirements.title: Session show subcommand
requirements.spec: WHEN the user runs 'norn session show <ID|NAME>', the system SHALL look up the session by full ID, partial prefix (minimum 8 characters), or name and display its metadata: ID, name, model, working directory, created/updated timestamps, event count, and status. IF the session is not found, the system SHALL exit with code 1 and print an error message.
acceptance: 'norn session show <full-uuid>' displays session metadata
'norn session show <8-char-prefix>' resolves by partial ID
'norn session show refactor-auth' resolves by name
Ambiguous partial prefix (matches multiple sessions) produces an error listing the candidates
Non-existent session produces 'Session not found: <input>' on stderr with exit code 1

requirements.title: Session resume subcommand
requirements.spec: WHEN the user runs 'norn session resume <ID|NAME>', the system SHALL load the session's events from the JSONL file, reconstruct the EventStore and message history, and enter the REPL with the resumed session. This is functionally equivalent to 'norn --resume <ID>'.
acceptance: 'norn session resume <ID>' loads the session and enters the REPL with prior context
The resumed session's history is visible to the agent in subsequent turns
Non-existent session ID produces an error and exits with code 1

requirements.title: Session fork subcommand
requirements.spec: WHEN the user runs 'norn session fork <ID|NAME>', the system SHALL copy the source session's events into a new session file, append a Fork event, and enter the REPL with the forked session. The new session gets a new UUID v7 ID. This is functionally equivalent to 'norn --fork <ID>'.
acceptance: 'norn session fork <ID>' creates a new session with copied events and enters the REPL
The forked session has a new ID distinct from the source
A Fork event is present in the new session's event list
The source session is not modified

requirements.title: Session export subcommand
requirements.spec: WHEN the user runs 'norn session export <ID|NAME>', the system SHALL serialize the session to stdout. IF --format is 'jsonl' (default), the system SHALL output the raw JSONL events. IF --format is 'json', the system SHALL output a single JSON object with session metadata and events array. IF --format is 'markdown', the system SHALL render a human-readable markdown transcript with user and assistant messages formatted as dialogue.
acceptance: 'norn session export <ID>' outputs JSONL to stdout by default
'norn session export <ID> --format json' outputs a JSON object
'norn session export <ID> --format markdown' outputs readable markdown
Markdown format shows user/assistant turns as headed sections
Output is valid for redirection to a file (stdout only, no stderr mixing)

requirements.title: Session remove subcommand
requirements.spec: WHEN the user runs 'norn session remove <ID|NAME>', the system SHALL delete the session's JSONL file and remove its entry from the session index. The system SHALL print 'Removed session <ID>.' to stderr. IF the session is not found, the system SHALL exit with code 1.
acceptance: 'norn session remove <ID>' deletes the session file and index entry
'Removed session <ID>.' is printed to stderr
The session no longer appears in 'norn session list'
Non-existent session produces an error with exit code 1

requirements.title: Auth login subcommand
requirements.spec: WHEN the user runs 'norn auth login', the system SHALL invoke norn::provider::auth::login() with LoginConfig::default(). The login flow and every other CLI auth surface SHALL resolve the same Norn-owned credential root through NORN_HOME; the login command SHALL NOT accept a path override. The login flow opens the system browser for OAuth PKCE authentication. On success, the system SHALL print 'Login successful.' to stderr. On failure, the system SHALL print the error and exit with code 3.
acceptance: 'norn auth login' triggers the OAuth PKCE flow (browser opens)
'norn auth login --codex-home /tmp/test' and 'norn auth login --auth-root /tmp/test' are rejected
Successful login prints 'Login successful.' to stderr and exits with code 0
Failed login prints error details and exits with code 3

requirements.title: Auth logout subcommand
requirements.spec: WHEN the user runs 'norn auth logout', the system SHALL invoke norn::provider::auth::logout() with the default LoginConfig. Local credential removal SHALL be attempted and durably completed before remote revocation starts. The system SHALL report local removal and remote revocation independently; a remote failure after local success SHALL state that the user is logged out locally and exit with code 3.
acceptance: 'norn auth logout' clears stored credentials
'Logged out.' is printed to stderr on success
Remote revoke failure does not restore the local credential and exits with code 3
Local removal failure is reported independently and exits with code 3

requirements.title: Auth status subcommand
requirements.spec: WHEN the user runs 'norn auth status', the system SHALL inspect Norn-owned $NORN_HOME/auth/auth.json (default ~/.norn/auth/auth.json) without mutating it and report one typed local state: missing, malformed, access-expired, refresh-candidate, locally-valid, or unknown. Known expiry MAY be shown. The system SHALL NOT display raw tokens or account identity and SHALL label remote validity as unverified.
acceptance: Missing credentials are reported as a non-error local state
Malformed or unusable credentials exit with code 3 and actionable remediation
Refreshable, locally-valid, and unknown-expiry credentials retain distinct reports
Raw access/refresh tokens and account identity are never displayed
Every state says remote validity is unverified

requirements.title: MCP serve subcommand
requirements.spec: WHEN the user runs 'norn mcp serve', the system SHALL construct an McpServer with the full ToolRegistry and call serve_stdio(). The server SHALL run on the process's stdin/stdout, handling JSON-RPC requests until EOF. The server name SHALL be 'norn' and the version SHALL match the crate version.
acceptance: 'norn mcp serve' starts an MCP server on stdio
Sending an 'initialize' JSON-RPC request receives a valid response with server info
Sending 'tools/list' returns all registered tools
Sending 'tools/call' dispatches the named tool and returns the result
The server exits cleanly on EOF (stdin closes)

requirements.title: MCP connect subcommand
requirements.spec: WHEN the user runs 'norn mcp connect <URI>', the system SHALL establish a connection to the MCP server at the given URI, send an 'initialize' handshake, then send 'tools/list' to enumerate the server's capabilities. The system SHALL print the server name, version, protocol version, and a list of available tools to stderr. IF the connection fails, the system SHALL print the error and exit with code 1.
acceptance: 'norn mcp connect stdio://path/to/server' connects and prints server info
Available tools from the remote server are listed with names and descriptions
Connection failure produces an error message and exit code 1
The connection is closed after enumeration (this is a test/probe, not a persistent connection)

requirements.title: Doctor subcommand
requirements.spec: WHEN the user runs 'norn doctor', the system SHALL perform the following checks and report pass/fail for each with actionable remediation messages: (1) Auth status: classify local OAuth credentials without claiming remote validity. (2) Provider connectivity: attempt an HTTP HEAD or lightweight request to the provider API endpoint to verify reachability. (3) Working directory permissions: verify the current directory is readable and writable. Each check SHALL print a status line to stderr in the format '[PASS] description' or '[FAIL] description: remediation'.
acceptance: 'norn doctor' prints status for auth, connectivity, and permissions checks
Each check shows [PASS] or [FAIL] with a clear description
[FAIL] lines include actionable remediation advice (e.g., 'Run `norn auth login` to authenticate.')
Exit code is 0 if all checks pass, 1 if any check fails

requirements.title: Completion subcommand
requirements.spec: WHEN the user runs 'norn completion <SHELL>' where SHELL is one of bash, zsh, or fish, the system SHALL generate a shell completion script via clap_complete and write it to stdout. The completion script SHALL cover all top-level flags, subcommands, and subcommand flags defined in the clap argument structure.
acceptance: 'norn completion bash' outputs a valid bash completion script to stdout
'norn completion zsh' outputs a valid zsh completion script to stdout
'norn completion fish' outputs a valid fish completion script to stdout
The generated script includes completions for all subcommands and flags
Output is suitable for redirection to a shell config file (e.g., 'norn completion zsh > ~/.zsh/_norn')

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement session persistence internals (JSONL read/write, index management) -- that is NC-002; this brief uses the NC-002 API
SHALL NOT implement the interactive REPL loop -- that is NC-005; session resume and fork dispatch into the REPL
SHALL NOT implement streaming display -- that is NC-007
SHALL NOT implement tool execution logic -- MCP serve delegates to the existing McpServer and ToolRegistry
SHALL NOT implement profile management subcommands -- per DESIGN.md, profile management via subcommand is premature
SHALL NOT expose raw OAuth tokens in auth status output
verification: cargo clippy -p norn-cli -- -D warnings produces zero warnings
cargo fmt -p norn-cli -- --check produces no diff
cargo test -p norn-cli passes all tests
'norn session list' shows sessions (or 'No sessions found.')
'norn session export <ID> --format markdown' outputs readable markdown
'norn auth login' triggers the OAuth flow
'norn auth status' reports login state without exposing tokens
'norn doctor' runs all health checks and reports results
'norn completion zsh' outputs a valid completion script
No file exceeds 500 lines of code
No code in mod.rs beyond pub mod declarations and re-exports
